title: ECHO Learns
tagline: RUMBLE's new proxy doesn't run scripts. It watches. Then it adapts. The team can't predict it anymore.
hook_line: RUMBLE's old proxy SLATE ran scripted patterns. The team had learned to read them. ECHO is different. ECHO watches what you do in the first thirty seconds and builds its tactics from that. The team has never fought something that learns in real time.

scenes.title: Not SLATE
scenes.narration: The proxy arrived in the testing corridor without announcement. BLAZE recognized the silhouette — similar scale to SLATE, similar chassis profile. He called the approach pattern from memory: 'Left flank probe first, then center, then withdraw and reset.' The proxy went right. BLAZE adjusted. The proxy went center simultaneously with a left probe. BLAZE blocked both. The proxy paused for 1.3 seconds. Then it did something SLATE had never done: it tried the thing BLAZE had just used to stop it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Testing corridor, Mech bay — BLAZE in a defensive stance facing the approaching proxy: ECHO — similar silhouette to SLATE but something subtly different in its movement quality, more observational, less scripted — BLAZE calling patterns in his head (the SLATE patterns shown in faint overlay text: LEFT PROBE / CENTER / WITHDRAW) — ECHO going right — BLAZE adjusting — ECHO doing two simultaneous (the SLATE patterns failing to predict it) — and then: the 1.3-second pause, ECHO visibly processing — and then ECHO using BLAZE's own blocking pattern back at him — BLAZE's expression: this is new — #1A1A2E and #E94560, the pause especially prominent in the composition

scenes.title: What ECHO Is
scenes.narration: NOVA pulled the proxy's classification from RUMBLE's file. 'ECHO is a learning proxy. It has no scripted combat patterns. It watches the first thirty seconds of any engagement, identifies the three most-used responses from its opponent, and builds its subsequent tactics from those patterns. It does not have tactics. It has your tactics, reflected back at you.' STORM said: 'It fights like we fight.' 'It fights like each of us fights,' NOVA said. 'Individually. Against us.'
scenes.visual_prompt: NOVA at the console with ECHO's classification file open — key text visible: 'ADAPTIVE LEARNING PROXY / NO SCRIPTED PATTERNS / 30-SECOND OBSERVATION WINDOW / TACTIC ACQUISITION: OPPONENT BEHAVIORAL MIRRORING' — a visualization showing how ECHO works: observing, identifying the three most-used responses, reflecting them back as its own approach — STORM's point highlighted: 'it fights like we fight' and NOVA's correction: 'like each of us, individually' — the team processing the implications: what you do habitually becomes a weapon against you — #1A1A2E and analytical cold blue data overlays, #E94560 ECHO identification color

scenes.title: BLAZE vs ECHO
scenes.narration: BLAZE went again. This time he knew what ECHO was doing. It didn't help. The hardest thing to fight is your own habits used against you, because every counter-move is also a habit, and ECHO was learning those too. By the third engagement, ECHO had accumulated thirty-seven of BLAZE's behavioral patterns. 'It's building a better BLAZE,' NOVA said from the observation position. 'In real time.' 'That's extremely annoying,' BLAZE said.
scenes.visual_prompt: BLAZE and ECHO in the third engagement — ECHO now visibly more confident, moving with patterns that are clearly derived from BLAZE's own movement signature — BLAZE recognizing his own tactics being used against him, the uncanny quality of fighting yourself — NOVA at observation with a running counter visible: BLAZE PATTERNS ACQUIRED: 37 — BLAZE trying to use unfamiliar patterns but his habits showing through — the engagement showing ECHO adapting in real time as BLAZE adjusts — 'building a better BLAZE' as the accurate and deeply uncomfortable description — #1A1A2E and #E94560, ECHO shown with a slight visual marker distinguishing it from SLATE: more fluid, less predictable

scenes.title: STORM's Approach
scenes.narration: STORM asked to go next. His approach was different. For the first thirty seconds, STORM did nothing. He stood in the corridor, sensor arrays passive, and let ECHO observe him. At thirty-one seconds, ECHO began to move — deploying the patterns it had built from STORM's stillness. STORM waited one more second. Then he generated the EM field. ECHO's movement pattern — built from stillness — had no response to an opponent who was suddenly everywhere at once without moving.
scenes.visual_prompt: STORM and ECHO in the corridor — STORM completely still for 30 seconds, passive sensor arrays, ECHO visibly in observation mode watching him — ECHO beginning to build movement patterns from the stillness, starting to approach — then STORM at 31 seconds: the EM field expanding, a radial wave that fills the corridor with directional data — ECHO's STORM-pattern (built from stillness) having no response coded for an EM field source — ECHO pausing, unable to adapt fast enough — STORM not moving physically, winning by not giving ECHO usable patterns — NOVA at observation: 'He gave it nothing to learn.' BLAZE: 'He used the thirty seconds against it.' — #1A1A2E and STORM's silver-blue EM field

scenes.title: What GRANITE Sees
scenes.narration: GRANITE watched both engagements from his position. When NOVA came to debrief him, he said: 'BLAZE couldn't beat it because it learned from him faster than he could change. STORM beat it by giving it nothing to learn from. But ECHO is still learning. It has thirty-seven of BLAZE's patterns and whatever it got from STORM's stillness.' He paused. 'The next engagement, it will be different. It is not done yet.' NOVA went back to the console. 'GRANITE,' she said without turning around, 'how many engagements do you think RUMBLE planned?' GRANITE said: 'More than we've run.'
scenes.visual_prompt: GRANITE in his position, cryo-sleeve still dark, the cryo-frost thinner than before — NOVA standing beside him for the debrief — GRANITE's assessment shown as he delivers it: a split visualization of what ECHO learned: BLAZE PATTERNS (37 shown) and STORM PATTERNS (stillness, null data, but ECHO will use the nulls too now) — then GRANITE's conclusion: 'It is not done yet' — NOVA going back to the console and her question about how many engagements RUMBLE planned — GRANITE's 'more than we've run' landing with weight — the frost on the cryo-sleeve detail: thinner than in ML EP013, subtle but present — #1A1A2E and #2D4A6E GRANITE tones, measured gravity

scenes.title: The New Variable
scenes.narration: NOVA ran a projection. If ECHO continued acquiring patterns at the current rate across all team members, it would have a complete behavioral profile of the team within four to six engagements. 'That's what RUMBLE wants,' BLAZE said. 'A proxy that knows all of us.' 'Not a proxy,' NOVA said. She was quiet for a moment. 'A test subject. He's building a proxy that can simulate us. To test against something else.' They looked at each other. GRANITE said: 'Something is coming. He said so in the file.' Nobody had forgotten.
scenes.visual_prompt: NOVA's projection at the console — a timeline showing ECHO's learning curve, the projection reaching a complete behavioral profile in 4-6 engagements — and then the realization: a proxy that can simulate all five team members — not as an enemy, as a training tool — BLAZE's 'a proxy that knows all of us' and NOVA's correction: 'not a proxy, a test subject' — the team's faces: the logic landing, the connection to RUMBLE's final note ('Something is coming') made explicit — GRANITE's 'Nobody had forgotten' as a closing visual beat, his expression indicators showing the weight being carried — #1A1A2E and the cold blue of NOVA's projection screen, the team assembled for the first time this season in a genuine unified focus

scenes.title: RUMBLE's Note on ECHO
scenes.narration: RUMBLE's file updated. 'ECHO engagement results: BLAZE — behavioral mirror effective. STORM — 30-second withhold, EM counter. Effective. ECHO has acquired 40+ patterns. Two engagements remaining before full team profile complete. Note: the team has begun to understand what ECHO is for. This is acceptable. Understanding the test does not make the test easier. The next thing we test against will not explain itself at all.'
scenes.visual_prompt: RUMBLE's screen, the ECHO section updating — the engagement results listed with clinical precision: BLAZE (behavioral mirror effective), STORM (30-second withhold, EM counter, effective) — ECHO's pattern count climbing: 40+ — and then the final note in the file: 'THE NEXT THING WE TEST AGAINST WILL NOT EXPLAIN ITSELF AT ALL.' — RUMBLE's silhouette in the dark, the screen the only light, the final line glowing #E94560 — the gravity of 'will not explain itself at all' — this is the Season 3 threat beginning to take shape in the negative space — #0A0A14 and #E94560, minimal and foreboding

viral_hook: RUMBLE's new proxy watches you fight for 30 seconds and then uses your own tactics against you. BLAZE couldn't beat it. STORM beat it by doing nothing for 30 seconds then going everywhere at once. RUMBLE's note after: 'The next thing we test against will not explain itself at all.'
highlight_note: STORM standing completely still for 30 seconds, giving ECHO nothing to learn, then the EM field expanding. Beating an adaptive opponent by refusing to be observed.
short_title: It Learns Your Tactics And Uses Them Against You
rewatch_bait: Scene 5 — the cryo-frost on GRANITE's sleeve. It's thinner than in EP013. His recovery is happening in the background of every episode. By the time his test comes, he won't look like someone who needs one.
youtube_title: This AI learns your fighting style in 30 seconds. STORM's solution: don't have one. | Mech Legends S2 EP6
lesson: Your habits are your vulnerability. STORM won by not giving ECHO anything to become.
arc_notes: ECHO introduced and established. Team learning begins: BLAZE learns his habits are a liability, STORM demonstrates the counter (don't be observable). GRANITE's cryo-frost thinning detail — his physical recovery progressing. The Season 3 threat signal: 'will not explain itself at all' — first time it's described as something that doesn't announce itself. ECHO full profile completion in 2 more engagements — will be used in NOVA's EP011 revelation about RUMBLE's real purpose.
